body.title: Stale-ticket sweeper — auto-/review pending tickets after N weeks
body.result: lab/claudecode/stale_ticket_sweeper.py — scans queue.json for pending tix aged past threshold (default 4wk) + gated tix where gate text mentions shipped ticket id. Dry-run yields candidates; real run writes log to ~/.TheIgors/claudecode/logs/stale_sweep_YYYY-MM-DD.md + one-liner to today's slate ## Ad hoc. Caught 5 legit stale gates on first run, ungated them. --include-gated flag enables gated-check. No auto-/review (judgment call). Can be wired to cron or Igor push_source later.
body.description:
Tickets that sit pending > N weeks (default 4) auto-route to /review with context 'is this still real? premise rotted? decision reversed? shipped under different ticket?'

Also checks gated tickets: 'is the gate still real? is the milestone dead?'

## Schedule
Run via Igor cron or CC skill on session start. Emits a report to slate ## Ad hoc if any findings.

## Blocked by
- T-review-as-filing-time-skill (so sweeper can call /review)

## Not in scope
- Auto-closing stale tickets — only flags, never closes.
